question: What is the design life of a NuScale Power Module?

nuscale_ch01_0050.title: Diesel Generator Buildings
nuscale_ch01_0050.summary: The Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs) in the NuScale Power Plant each house a single backup diesel generator and their auxiliary equipment, but do not contain any safety-related systems or support emergency safety functions. Their primary role is to provide support and housing for the generators, associated equipment, and maintenance areas.

nuscale_ch01_0049.title: Central Utility Building
nuscale_ch01_0049.summary: The Central Utility Building (CUB) is a nonsafety-related structure that houses essential utility services for the plant, such as chiller equipment, air systems, water treatment, maintenance, and security functions. Additionally, the plant includes Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs) for backup power, and its design incorporates human factors engineering to minimize errors and optimize control and automation.

nuscale_ch01_0036.title: Refueling Operations
nuscale_ch01_0036.summary: Refueling operations for a NuScale Power Module (NPM) are conducted independently from other modules. The process involves moving the NPM from its operating bay to the refueling pool, disassembling it for core refueling and maintenance, and then reassembling and returning it to service after inspections and tests are completed.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides an overview of the NuScale plant design, summarizing key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, waste management, and auxiliary systems. It explains that each Combined License (COL) applicant must reference the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) and provide site-specific details where required, with certain conceptual design information and security details clearly identified and delineated.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section explains that the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report includes simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It references tables and figures listing and explaining the symbols used in these diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0035.title: Fuel Handling and Reactor Maintenance Areas
nuscale_ch01_0035.summary: The fuel handling and reactor maintenance areas are located at the west end of the Reactor Building and include spaces for the spent fuel pool, refueling pool, and dry dock, all designed to facilitate safe fuel handling and reactor maintenance operations. These areas provide equipment and access for refueling, fuel assembly transport, and temporary storage of both new and spent fuel, while ensuring radiation protection for personnel.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and identification of text, tables, figures, and page numbering within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR). It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, some sensitive content is withheld from public disclosure in accordance with regulations, and a separate public version is provided with such material removed.

nuscale_ch01_0012.title: Page Numbering
nuscale_ch01_0012.summary: Tables and figures in the FSAR are numbered sequentially within each section and placed at the end of the relevant section, while pages are numbered according to their section or appendix designation. The FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, but sensitive material is withheld from public disclosure and clearly marked, with a separate public version provided.

nuscale_ch01_0123.title: Table 1.9-2: Conformance with Regulatory Guides (Continued)
nuscale_ch01_0123.summary: This section of the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report summarizes the reactor's conformance with various NRC Regulatory Guides. It details whether NuScale's design and quality assurance programs fully, partially, or do not conform to each guide, providing explanations and references to relevant report sections for each criterion. Some guides are marked as not applicable due to differences in standards or design approaches.

nuscale_ch01_0455.title: DSRS 11.1, Rev 0: Coolant Source TermsII.2 RG 1.112
nuscale_ch01_0455.summary: This section explains that several regulatory guidelines for auxiliary feedwater systems and steam generator designs are not applicable to the NuScale plant because its design differs significantly from large pressurized water reactors (PWRs). Specifically, NuScale uses a passive Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) instead of pump-driven auxiliary feedwater systems, and its steam generator design avoids the configurations and associated risks (such as water hammer) addressed by these guidelines.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, core design, and safety systems, showing that NuScale uses a smaller, simpler, and more integrated design with fewer components and lower power output per module compared to conventional PWRs. The comparison is summarized in tables for clarity.

edges.description: Section A provides a general overview of the plant's systems and structures, including those described in Section B, which details specific utility and safety-related functions of the Diesel Generator Buildings and Central Utility Building.
Section B provides specific details about the Central Utility Building, which is one of the facility components generally described in Section A's summary of the overall plant design.

Section A describes specific refueling operations for a NuScale Power Module, which are detailed procedures that fall under the broader general plant description and systems overview provided in Section B.

Section A describes the operational procedures for refueling a NuScale Power Module, while Section B provides supporting drawings and figures that visually clarify design information referenced in Section A, such as component positions during operations.

Section A describes the process of moving a NuScale Power Module (NPM) for refueling, while Section B details the infrastructure (pipe fittings and connections) that enables such operations, connecting them through the operational prerequisites for NPM handling.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant design, while Section B offers detailed drawings that visually clarify and specify the systems described in Section A, including those relevant to the design life of the NuScale Power Module.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant design, while Section B offers specific details about the fuel handling and reactor maintenance areas, thereby specifying aspects of the general description in Section A.

Section A describes the general structure and regulatory basis of the FSAR, while Section B provides operational details about refueling, with both sections being connected by their roles in documenting and supporting the overall design and operation of the NuScale Power Module as required by regulatory guidance.

Section A describes the general structure and referencing system of the FSAR, while Section B provides specific content about the Central Utility Building, with Section A's guidance enabling the organization and identification of Section B's information.

Section A lists regulatory conformance for safety-related structures, systems, and components (SSCs), while Section B describes the Central Utility Building as a nonsafety-related structure, connecting them through the classification of plant structures by safety significance.

Section A references regulatory conformance criteria for safety-related structures, while Section B describes the Central Utility Building as a nonsafety-related structure, connecting them through the classification of plant structures by safety significance.

Section A outlines regulatory conformance, including quality assurance and seismic design criteria, which establish the foundational requirements that directly impact the design life of the NuScale Power Module discussed in Section B's operational context.

Section A lists regulatory guides with which the NuScale design conforms or partially conforms, while Section B discusses the applicability of specific design guidelines and regulatory guides to the NuScale module, both providing evidence of regulatory conformance relevant to establishing the design life.

Section B provides nominal design parameters for the NuScale Power Module, which may include design life information, while Section A lists regulatory guide conformance but does not directly address design life, making Section B a potential source of the specific design life value referenced in the analysis question.

Section B provides nominal design parameters for the NuScale Power Module, which may include design life information, while Section A discusses regulatory guidance and its applicability, thus Section B offers specific data that could satisfy regulatory requirements referenced or discussed in Section A.
